summary: PR 2306 renamed the effect translation unit from `ef_061D` to `efspecial` and renamed the function symbol `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`, updating splits, symbols, build configuration, header/source names, include references, and the call from `efSync_Spawn`. The remaining touched C files contain formatting/include-order-only changes shown in the diff.

key_files.role: Updated split unit path from `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c` for the 0x80061D70 effect unit.
Renamed symbol at `.text:0x80061D70` from `ef_80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` while preserving size and scope metadata.
Updated Matching object path from `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c`.
Renamed source file from `ef_061D.c`, updated include to `efspecial.h`, and renamed function definition to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Renamed header from `ef_061D.h` and updated prototype to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Updated extern declaration and call/comment in `efSync_Spawn` to use `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`.
Formatting-only line wrapping changes around `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls.
Formatting-only line wrapping changes around two `it_8027ADEC` calls.
Formatting-only line wrapping changes around local attribute assignment and velocity expression.

what_changed: Renamed split entry `melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `melee/ef/efspecial.c` while preserving text/data/sdata/sdata2 address ranges.
Renamed symbol `ef_80061D70` at `.text:0x80061D70` to `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` with the same function size `0x1BC0` and global scope.
Renamed `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.c` to `src/melee/ef/efspecial.c` and changed its self-include from `ef_061D.h` to `efspecial.h`.
Renamed `src/melee/ef/ef_061D.h` to `src/melee/ef/efspecial.h` and updated the function prototype.
Updated `efsync.c` extern declaration and callsite for the renamed function.
Made small formatting/include-order changes in Kirby and item files without visible semantic changes in the diff.

smart_moves.text: The rename was applied across split metadata, symbol metadata, build configuration, owning source/header files, and at least one callsite, reducing the chance of a partial TU/symbol rename.
The symbol rename kept the original address, size, and scope comment intact, making the change traceable as a name update rather than a function relocation.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For a translation-unit rename, update the split entry, configure object path, source/header filenames, self-include, symbol table name, prototype, externs, and callsites together.

Rename-only PRs can produce report noise that appears as new matches under the new unit name and broken matches under the old unit name; intake should preserve both sides of that evidence rather than treating it as a normal code-match improvement without context.

naming_conventions.name: efspecial
naming_conventions.evidence: PR renamed `ef_061D.c`/`.h` and split/configure entries to `efspecial.c`/`.h`.

naming_conventions.name: efSpecial_SpawnSpecial
naming_conventions.evidence: PR renamed address-style function `ef_80061D70` at 0x80061D70 to semantic name `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` in symbols, prototype, definition, extern, and callsite.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: Keep split ranges and symbol address/size metadata unchanged while renaming the unit and symbol, so matching/report tooling can associate the same bytes with the new names.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Report showed 4 new matches for `main/melee/ef/efspecial` (`efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`, `.data`, `.sdata2`, `.sdata`) and 4 broken matches for the old `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` names.

follow_up_queries: rename old unit broken matches new unit matches

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The diff strongly supports the rename and metadata updates, but does not explain why the semantic name `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial` was chosen.
No independent build or objdiff output is loaded beyond the decomp-dev bot report; report evidence is sufficient only to describe reported new/broken matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Coordinate TU and symbol renames across metadata, build config, declarations, and callsites

accepted_candidate_records.title: Rename PR reports may show old-unit breakage paired with new-unit matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report for PR 2306 lists `main/melee/ef/efspecial` as newly matched and `main/melee/ef/ef_061D` as broken for the same code/data byte counts, consistent with a unit rename rather than standalone text-section progress.

rejection_notes: Do not infer the naming rationale for `efSpecial_SpawnSpecial`; the loaded PR has no body or human comments explaining it.
Do not treat the formatting-only changes in fighter/item files as decomp lessons beyond noting that they were present.
Do not claim a new code match from this PR without rename context; the bot report pairs new `efspecial` matches with broken old `ef_061D` matches.
